Here’s the run down by our compound protocol lead @calebkaup

Proposal 109 lowers the reserve factors for 10 tokens on Compound. Though this may help to incentivize more supply, demand is weighing right now. Gauntlet points out that the protocol is well covered in a Black Thursday magnitude event, so keeping reserves so high to cover for this is unnecessary. However, as @sirrokko covers here (Gauntlet Reserve Factor Optimization Framework - #6 by Sirokko) the protocol ought to be focused on maximizing its revenue beyond pure insolvency risk. This proposal would slow the rate of reserve growth (and raise supply rates), which is not ideal at this time.

We think that adding a formal analysis period will help make the governance more robust and secure by providing ample time to audit and for people to change their COMP allocation before the vote goes through.
I suppose this proposal because it seems like a good idea to improve the oracle.
While I like the idea of this proposal, I worry that it opens the door of potentially leading to a centralization of the protocol down the line.
